MODULE ONE – Your Avatar, Part 1

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
• 	Your avatar is a composite representation of your ideal client - who is that?
• 	It is very helpful to actually think of your avatar as a real person. You can even give her/him a
name (I recommend this). Remember, you are always presenting/writing to one person.
• 	At Internet Alchemy (my company) we don’t ever send a “BLAST” to our list. PLF is all about
CONNECTING with your prospects and clients, so “blasting” them would not make sense -
they are people!
• 	Initially I thought it was about creating incredible marketing by connecting with prospects.
But now, I see that it’s about having a greater/stronger connection using incredible marketing.
• 	So how do you do that? The first step in connecting with them is understanding them.
• 	This next set of questions will give you the insights you need to create your offer - they’ll be the
fuel for your launch. You need to picture your avatar in your mind’s eye.
• 	The Questions:
o 	What’s your avatar’s biggest source of pain?
o 	What keeps them awake at night? What scares your avatar?
o 	What is the biggest danger that they don’t even see yet?
o 	What’s your avatar’s greatest opportunities?
o 	Do they have opportunities that they don’t even know about yet?
o 	What are your avatar’s hopes and dreams?
o 	Who or what do they aspire to be?
o 	Now zoom in on your avatar’s needs...
o 	Why do they want/don’t want _________?
o 	What are the signs they have/don’t have _________?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

• 	Once you do this, make sure to record the calls and get them transcribed. You can do this on
sites like InternetTranscribers.com.
• 	After going through the Asset Identification exercise, you should have thought of some assets
you already have. What are they? Write them down!
• 	The key is to start on all the long term stuff immediately. Start publishing on social media.
Start building a presence in forums in your niche. Identify top blogs and start making
intelligent comments.

TRANSCRIPT:
We’re now going to talk about your avatar, and this is some of the most
important, most powerful work you’ll do within this module and probably within the
entire Product Launch Formula.
Your avatar, is a composite representation of your ideal client.
Who is your average client? Sometimes you might have more than one avatar.
For example, in Product Launch Formula I have more than one avatar. I have people
that already have an existing online business and they want to leverage it. They want
to get better results.
I also have people that want to start an online business and are just starting
out. Those are two very different avatars. I've got more avatars - artists, authors,
practitioners, people with offline businesses that want to move online. So as you can
see I have several different avatars.
If you have more than one avatar (and you probably do) you might want to do
this exercise we're about to go through for each avatar. This is really powerful stuff.
This concept of being really clear on who you're trying to reach is super important.
It can be really helpful to think of your avatar as a real person. I
know some people recommend giving them a real name, and talk about them as if
they're a specific person. That can be really powerful because in your marketing
you always want to be talking to one person.
You don't want to be writing to your list or recording videos thinking you’re
writing an email to 6,000 people. No, you're writing that email for 6,000 individual
people who are going to go check their email, real individual people who are going to
open up their email program and see your email in their inbox. This is a very private
place for them, and they're going to read it to see if you connect with them.
It’s the same thing when people watch your video. You might be putting it out
on YouTube, or using a prelaunch video that lots of people will watch, but it’s
individual people watching it. Never forget that you're talking to one person.
A lot of times people talk like they’re going to “send out blasts.” “I'm going to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

MODULE ONE – Your Avatar, Part 1
5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
blast my list, I'm going to send a blast to my list” and they're basically talking about
email broadcasts, but they'll use that word blast. Some people just use it and that's
fine, but we have a rule in my company with my team. We can't use that word,
"blast." We do not blast the people on our list. Do you like to get blasted?
This is more of a mindset idea, but you never want to blast your list. You're
sending emails to your individual subscribers. It's all about connecting with
your prospects and clients - that’s what PLF is all about at the end of the
day.
The first step in connecting with them is to understand who they
are, and that's what this whole process is about. I'm going to go through this
quickly, but this is not a process that you should go through quickly. You should
spend some real time walking through this process.
This is how you connect with your avatar. I've got a set of questions for you,
it's going to give you some real insights that will help you create your offer. It’ll be the
fuel for your launch, it'll help you create every piece of content for them - whether it's
an email, or a blog post, or a video etc. You need to picture who your avatar is. You
have to get a real clear picture of them in your mind's eye.
I want you to actually work on these questions right now...
Think about your avatar, your ideal client. Who you want to reach? The
person you want to be a hero to…
• 	What is their biggest source of pain? What keeps them awake at
night? Just sit there and think about that. What's their biggest source
of pain? What do they worry about when they lie awake at night?
• 	What scares your avatar? This can be a little different. If we go
back, source of pain, what scares them, could be very, very similar but
they could be different. What scares your avatar?
• 	Then what's the biggest danger that they don't even see
yet? You probably have greater insight into what their path or
journey is, what their transformation in life is going to be, and maybe
they've got something coming and they don't even know about that
danger yet.

• 	What are your avatar's greatest opportunities? What's out
there for them? What awaits them? I think about PLF and your
opportunities ahead of you, and you might think OK, I just want to
knock out this launch, and I want to focus on that. I also want to focus
on you having that launch, but then I can look into your future,
because I've already been there.
I started out and I just look at my journey and I know that my journey is your
journey, and I can think about the amazing things, not just in your first launch, but
the launch after that and the launch after that. The business that comes out of that,
and then the next business and all the connections you're going to have.
I can just think about these amazing opportunities that you don't even know
about yet. When you think about your avatar, what are the opportunities they don't
even know about yet that are just going to show up? People don't even know about
the launch echo when they first hear about PLF, but I know about it. I know about
the positioning that's ahead of them.
I want you to do the same thing. I want you to think about what their
opportunities are going forward. So go ahead, write that down now.
• 	What are your avatar's hopes and dreams?
• 	Who or what do they aspire to be?
• 	What's just a huge dream for them?
As you work through these questions and spend some time, you're going to
get really clear on who your avatar is and that's going to allow you to connect with
them through everything you do - through your video, your copy, your blog posts,
emails, and I can’t even tell you how powerful this is.
This is what I've built my entire business on and is my secret,
being able to truly connect with people. It's the basic ingredients of how you
connect with your prospects and your clients.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
